rulings.Case Title: THOMAS FORCE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (exposure/causation) and granted as to issues 2 and 3 (false representation and punitive damages). The declarations of plaintiff and Dr. Bruch and defendant¿s responses to GO 129 interrogatories create triable issues whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos from painter¿s caulk and architectural caulk attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Dr. Bruch are overruled. Plaintiff appears to concede that his claim for false representation and request for punitive damages lack merit. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS FORCE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-6 and 8 (exposure/product identification, bulk supplier defense, sophisticated user defense, and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 7 (false representation). The declaration of plaintiff and deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. The D¿Amico objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. The purportedly inconsistent deposition testimony cited by defendant only concerns plaintiff¿s use of the red dot product, not the use of the red dot product by others. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). The sophisticated user defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. Plaintiff submitted more than ample evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his cause of action for false representation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TIM LEHFELDT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to any asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD BOLLINGER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production to show that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bollinger has an asbestos-related disease. By not referring to any responses by plaintiffs to contention discovery in its separate statement, defendant is unable to successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did satisfy its burden of production, the declaration of Dr. Horn creates a triable issue whether Mr. Bollinger has an asbestos-related disease. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and civil battery and their request for punitive damages, the issues as to which defendant moved for summary adjudication. = (220/HEK)
